prompt: Kuukikan (空気感) style hyper-realistic film photograph. Same California suburban kitchen, evening 6:00 PM. On the wooden cutting board atop the kitchen counter, a fresh salmon fillet, broccoli florets, and garlic cloves are beautifully arranged — ready to cook. The Samsung Family Hub screen on the refrigerator (background, left side) shows a personalized dinner suggestion with checkmarks (blurred, out of focus). In the far background, a Japanese woman in her mid-30s (Yuki) sits at the dining table working on a laptop — she is small in the frame, calm, undistracted by dinner prep. Warm kitchen lighting from above blends with the last natural light from the window. The salmon surface glistens softly with moisture. Color grading: warm-toned, inviting. Natural food colors are beautiful but kept in a film-like, slightly desaturated palette. Shadows are luminous and transparent. Composition: 16:9 wide frame. Salmon and ingredients are the hero (center-right). Family Hub screen sits on the left. Yuki is small in the background. A sense of abundance and preparedness. Camera: 50mm prime lens equivalent, f/2.0, ISO 200. Fine film grain. The wood cutting board and salmon texture are sharp in focus. Background softly blurred with old-lens bokeh. The mood is 'this family's dinner is naturally ready' — a warm, reassuring sense that the system understands this household.
negative_prompt: dramatic lighting, lens flare, HDR, oversaturated, CGI, plastic look, studio food photography, commercial food styling, dark moody, text overlays, watermarks, logos, AI smoothing, digital painting, anime, cartoon, illustration
